---
title: "Platform Server Build Guide — Windows Server 2025 with Gitea, IIS, Intune, Azure Arc, and OrgTree"
subtitle: "Canonical 14-phase runbook for the UIAO on-premises governance substrate"
doc-type: runbook
doc-id: UIAO_SBG_001
version: "1.2"
status: DRAFT
audience: "Platform engineers · sysadmins"
canon-source: "inbox/Posted/UIAO Platform Server Build Guide — Windows Server 2025 with Gitea and IIS.docx (absent — Phase 1–13 PowerShell inlined here as interim authority)"
derived-from: "UIAO_SBG_001 v1.0 (Posted, missing) + OrgTree canon (UIAO_151–UIAO_176)"
boundary: "gcc-moderate (M365 SaaS); IaaS per ADR-041"
aspirational: true
---

# Platform Server Build Guide {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note title="Canon derivation"}
This page consolidates two canonical sources:

1. **UIAO Platform Server Build Guide** (`UIAO_SBG_001 v1.0`) — the original
   13-phase PowerShell-first runbook for Windows Server 2025, IIS, Gitea,
   Intune, and Azure Arc. The Posted `.docx` source (declared at
   `canon-source` above) is **currently absent from `inbox/Posted/`**; until
   it is restored, the Phase 1–13 procedures below are the interim
   operational authority on this page.
2. **OrgTree Modernization Canon** (`UIAO_151–UIAO_176`) — the governance
   model for OrgPath encoding, dynamic groups, Administrative Units, and
   drift detection. Phase 14 below instantiates this canon for the platform
   server.

This page also reconciles the architectural decision recorded in
**ADR-041 (UIAO Git Infrastructure)** — Gitea behind IIS (Option B) — against
the alternate IIS + `git-http-backend` build documented in
[`git-server-implementation.qmd`](./git-server-implementation.qmd) (Option A,
retained for lab/reference use; see Appendix A below).
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Aspirational — canonically declared, partially adopted

Phases 1–13 below are inlined from canon and validated against the Posted v1.0
outline; the operational `.docx` source is missing and these procedures should
be field-rehearsed before production cutover. Phase 14 OrgTree Integration is
canonically declared but operationally new; UIAO_163 drift detection hooks and
AU-scoped Gitea authorization are under development. ADR-041 (Git
Infrastructure) is currently `status: draft` in `src/uiao/canon/adr/` — finalize
before tagging this guide as production-ready.
:::

## Architecture in one page

A single Windows Server 2025 host runs Gitea on port 3000, fronted by IIS as
an HTTPS reverse proxy on 443. Authentication chains AD DS (LDAPS) for local
accounts and Entra ID (OAuth2/OIDC) for federated users. The host enrolls in
Intune for compliance telemetry and Azure Arc for hybrid management. As of
this revision, the host is also a first-class OrgTree object: it carries an
OrgPath, lives inside an Administrative Unit, and emits drift state to the
UIAO_163 engine.

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Windows Server 2025 (UIAO-GIT01)                                 │
│   OrgPath: ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM  (extensionAttribute1)            │
│   AU:      AU-ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM                                │
│                                                                  │
│   IIS :443 (HTTPS) ──reverse proxy──▶ Gitea :3000               │
│     │                                   │                        │
│     ├─ AD DS LDAPS (primary auth)       ├─ Repos on D:\GitRepos  │
│     ├─ Entra OIDC (federated SSO)       ├─ Hooks (governance)    │
│     └─ Client cert (mTLS optional)      └─ Intune compliance tag │
│                                                                  │
│   Azure Arc agent → drift reporting → UIAO_163 engine               │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Phase 0 — Prerequisites

These prerequisites must hold before Phase 1 begins. They consolidate every
runtime, module, IIS extension, and policy assumption the rest of the guide
depends on. Missing any one of them will cause a later phase to fail
non-obviously.

### 0.1 Host and OS

| Item | Required value | Verification |
|---|---|---|
| Host | Single Windows Server 2025 VM (build 26100+), Desktop Experience | `Get-ComputerInfo` |
| Hostname | `UIAO-GIT01` (or per OrgPath convention) | `$env:ComputerName` |
| CPU / RAM | 4 vCPU / 16 GB minimum (8 / 32 recommended) | `Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystem` |
| Disks | `C:` OS (120 GB), `D:` data (≥ 500 GB), `E:` logs/backup optional | `Get-Volume` |
| Domain join | Joined to legacy AD forest (least-privilege computer account) | `Test-ComputerSecureChannel` |
| Entra registration | Hybrid → device exists in tenant with `extensionAttribute1` placeholder | `Get-MgDevice -Filter "displayName eq 'UIAO-GIT01'"` |
| Time sync | NTP to forest PDC emulator (Kerberos requires ≤ 5 min skew) | `w32tm /query /status` |

### 0.2 PowerShell runtime

| Item | Required value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| PowerShell | **7.4+** (`pwsh`) installed alongside Windows PowerShell 5.1 | `https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/releases` — pinned MSI |
| Execution policy | `AllSigned` on the user scope; `RemoteSigned` permissible during build only | `Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope LocalMachine -ExecutionPolicy AllSigned` |
| PSGallery trust | Trusted, with `PSResourceGet` v1.0+ as the install transport | `Set-PSResourceRepository -Name PSGallery -Trusted` |
| Code-signing cert | Enterprise-CA-issued, Subject `CN=UIAO Canon Signing` | issued by ADCS template `UIAO-CodeSigning` |

::: {.callout-warning title="PowerShell version reconciliation"}
Earlier revisions of UIAO documentation inconsistently named **PS 5.1+** as
the floor (see `git-server-implementation.qmd:38`). The canonical floor is
**PowerShell 7.4+**, set by `01-platform-foundation.qmd:114` and by every
`#!/usr/bin/env pwsh` shebang in the Gitea hook set. Windows PowerShell 5.1
is retained only for `Install-WindowsFeature` and a small set of legacy IIS
cmdlets that have not been ported to PS 7; everything else runs on `pwsh`.
:::

### 0.3 PowerShell modules

All modules are installed from PSGallery (or an internal PSResource mirror —
see §0.5). Install with `Install-PSResource -Name <name> -Scope AllUsers`.

| Module | Min version | Used by | Phase |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ActiveDirectory` | RSAT 26100 | Forest reads, GPO/DNS/DHCP inventory | 6 |
| `GroupPolicy` | RSAT 26100 | GPO inventory adapter | 6 |
| `DnsServer` | RSAT 26100 | DNS-zone read adapter | 6 |
| `DhcpServer` | RSAT 26100 | DHCP-scope read adapter | 6 |
| `WebAdministration` | Built-in (Windows PowerShell 5.1) | IIS site/binding/pool admin | 2, 5, 9 |
| `IISAdministration` | Built-in (PS 7-compatible) | IIS preferred admin module | 2, 5 |
| `Microsoft.Graph.Authentication` | 2.19+ | Graph connect | 7, 10, 14 |
| `Microsoft.Graph.Identity.DirectoryManagement` | 2.19+ | AUs, devices, role assignments | 14.1, 14.2 |
| `Microsoft.Graph.Groups` | 2.19+ | Dynamic groups, group claims | 7, 14.3 |
| `Microsoft.Graph.DeviceManagement` | 2.19+ | Intune compliance policy | 10, 14.5 |
| `Az.Accounts` | 3.0+ | Azure sign-in | 11, 12 |
| `Az.Resources` | 7.0+ | RG/tag operations | 11, 14.1 |
| `Az.ConnectedMachine` | 1.0+ | Arc enrollment + tags | 11, 14.1, 14.4 |
| `Az.Storage` | 7.0+ | Nightly backup to Azure Blob | 12 |
| `Pester` | 5.5+ | Validation harness, Phase 0–13 smoke tests | 0, 13 |
| `PSPKI` | 4.2+ | ADCS template + ESC1-8 read (DM_020 adapter) | informational |
| `Microsoft.PowerShell.SecretManagement` | 1.1+ | Two-Brain credential split | 4, 11 |
| `Microsoft.PowerShell.SecretStore` | 1.0+ | Local secret vault for service accounts | 4, 11 |
| `PSScriptAnalyzer` | 1.22+ | Hook precondition for pre-receive validation | 9 |
| `UIAOADAssessment` (internal) | per release | Least-privilege forest reader | 6 |

### 0.4 Windows / IIS dependencies

`Install-WindowsFeature` covers the first group; the remaining items are
separate MSI installers that the IIS install media does **not** include.

```powershell
# 0.4.a Built-in Windows roles + features (idempotent)
Install-WindowsFeature -Name `
    Web-Server, Web-Common-Http, Web-Static-Content, Web-Default-Doc, `
    Web-Http-Errors, Web-Http-Redirect, `
    Web-App-Dev, Web-Net-Ext45, Web-Asp-Net45, `
    Web-Health, Web-Http-Logging, Web-Custom-Logging, Web-Request-Monitor, `
    Web-Security, Web-Filtering, Web-Windows-Auth, Web-Basic-Auth, `
    Web-Performance, Web-Stat-Compression, Web-Dyn-Compression, `
    Web-Mgmt-Console, Web-Scripting-Tools `
    -IncludeManagementTools
```

| MSI / extension | Source | Why required | Pin |
|---|---|---|---|
| **URL Rewrite Module 2.1** | `https://www.iis.net/downloads/microsoft/url-rewrite` (canonical download.microsoft.com path pinned in §0.4 PowerShell below) | Phase 5 reverse-proxy rewrite rules to Gitea on `:3000` | 7.2.1993 or later |
| **Application Request Routing 3.0** | `https://www.iis.net/downloads/microsoft/application-request-routing` | ARR enables the reverse proxy + health probes | 3.0.1985 or later |
| **HttpPlatformHandler 1.2** | `https://www.iis.net/downloads/microsoft/httpplatformhandler` | Required by `deploy/windows-server/web.config` for the FastAPI service | 1.2.0 |
| **.NET 8 hosting bundle** | `https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/8.0` | URL Rewrite, ARR, HttpPlatformHandler runtime | 8.0.x LTS |
| **Visual C++ 2015–2022 redist (x64)** | `https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe` | Required by Gitea on Windows + several PS native modules | latest |
| **Git for Windows** | `https://github.com/git-for-windows/git/releases/download/v2.44.0.windows.1/Git-2.44.0-64-bit.exe` | `git` CLI on the host for clone/fetch/hook execution | 2.44.0+ |
| **Gitea (Windows AMD64)** | `https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/releases/download/v1.21.11/gitea-1.21.11-windows-4.0-amd64.exe` | The canonical SSOT service per ADR-041 | 1.21.x LTS |
| **gpg4win** | `https://www.gpg4win.org/get-gpg4win.html` (mirror to internal feed for offline) | Pre-receive signed-commit verification (Phase 9) | 4.3+ |

::: {.callout-tip title="Source-of-truth pattern"}
Every binary above has a stable GitHub Releases or Microsoft-CDN URL. Pin the
exact version and the SHA-256 in a release manifest under
`canon/release-manifests/platform-server-v1.2.yaml` and verify with
`Get-FileHash` before installation. This is how the platform survives
upstream filename changes and supply-chain compromises both.
:::

### 0.5 Internal package mirrors (recommended for production)

For Tier-0 hosts the recommended pattern is **never reach the public
internet at runtime** — pre-stage every binary on an internal feed and
configure the host to consume only that feed.

| Public feed | Internal mirror pattern | Tool |
|---|---|---|
| PSGallery (`https://www.powershellgallery.com/api/v2`) | Internal `PSResourceGet` repository on Artifactory / Azure Artifacts | `Register-PSResourceRepository -Name UIAOPS -Uri https://artifactory.corp/repo/psgallery -Trusted` |
| nuget.org | `https://artifactory.corp/repo/nuget` | `nuget sources add` |
| GitHub Releases (binaries listed in §0.4) | Internal HTTPS mirror at `https://artifactory.corp/repo/github-releases/` | `Invoke-WebRequest` against the mirror URL |
| Docker Hub / `mcr.microsoft.com` | `https://artifactory.corp/repo/docker-hub/` and `/repo/mcr/` | `docker login` + image-name rewrite |

When a mirror is in use, replace every public URL in the Phase 1–13
PowerShell with the mirror equivalent and re-pin SHA-256 hashes in
`canon/release-manifests/platform-server-v1.2.yaml`.

### 0.6 Code-signing posture

WDAC and AppLocker (Phase 13) require every script executed on the host to
be signed. Issue a code-signing cert from the enterprise CA's
`UIAO-CodeSigning` template, install it into `Cert:\CurrentUser\My` of the
build account, and sign every `.ps1` under `D:\GitRepos\uiao\scripts\` and
every Gitea hook before deployment:

```powershell
$cert = Get-ChildItem Cert:\CurrentUser\My |
    Where-Object { $_.Subject -like '*UIAO Canon Signing*' } |
    Select-Object -First 1
Get-ChildItem -Path 'D:\GitRepos\uiao\scripts\' -Filter '*.ps1' -Recurse |
    ForEach-Object {
        Set-AuthenticodeSignature -FilePath $_.FullName -Certificate $cert `
            -TimestampServer 'http://timestamp.digicert.com'
    }
```

The pre-receive hook in Phase 9 rejects any unsigned script committed to the
canonical refs.

## Phase 0.5 — Egress allowlist for proxy-restricted environments

The Posted guide and earlier revisions of this page were silent on
network-egress policy. For federal / Tier-0 environments the platform server
typically sits behind an authenticated forward proxy with a deny-by-default
egress rule. The build and runtime require **outbound HTTPS (443)** to the
following hostnames; these are the entries the proxy/firewall team
allowlists. Nothing here is a "proxy bypass" — these are documented,
audited egress exceptions.

### 0.5.a Build-time (one-shot, while installing Phase 1–13)

| Hostname / pattern | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `github.com`, `objects.githubusercontent.com`, `codeload.github.com`, `raw.githubusercontent.com` | UIAO repo clone + GitHub Releases binaries (git, Gitea, PowerShell) |
| `*.powershellgallery.com`, `psg-prod-eastus.azureedge.net` | PowerShell module install (or replace with §0.5 internal mirror) |
| `aka.ms`, `download.microsoft.com`, `www.iis.net` | URL Rewrite, ARR, HttpPlatformHandler MSIs |
| `dotnet.microsoft.com`, `dotnetcli.azureedge.net` | .NET 8 hosting bundle |
| `packages.microsoft.com` | Azure CLI, optional `pwsh` upgrades |
| `timestamp.digicert.com`, `timestamp.sectigo.com` | Authenticode timestamping for code signing |
| Enterprise CA HTTP-CDP + OCSP URLs (per `Get-CACrlDistributionPoint`) | Certificate chain validation |

### 0.5.b Runtime (persistent, after cutover)

| Hostname / pattern | Purpose | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| `github.com`, `api.github.com` | Upstream canon mirror — scheduled `git fetch` | 8 |
| `login.microsoftonline.com`, `graph.microsoft.com` | Entra OIDC + Graph for Gitea OIDC and Phase 14 emitters | 7, 14 |
| `management.azure.com`, `*.guestconfiguration.azure.com`, `*.his.arc.azure.com` | Arc agent, Arc-projected policy + Guest Config | 11, 14.4 |
| `*.blob.core.windows.net` (RG-scoped) | Nightly Gitea backup target | 12 |
| `*.ods.opinsights.azure.com`, `*.oms.opinsights.azure.com` | Azure Monitor / Log Analytics workspace forwarding | 11, 13 |
| `*.events.data.microsoft.com` | Intune compliance attestation | 10, 14.5 |
| Internal LDAPS endpoints (`dc01.corp…`, `dc02.corp…`) on TCP/636 | Gitea AD `source-config` for legacy accounts | 6 |
| Enterprise CA AIA + OCSP URLs | TLS chain validation | 3 |

### 0.5.c Forward proxy configuration on the host

If the proxy requires Kerberos / NTLM auth, configure WinHTTP and PS at the
machine scope:

```powershell
# 0.5.c — Persist the corporate proxy for WinHTTP, BITS, .NET, and PowerShell
$proxy = 'http://proxy.corp.contoso.com:8080'
$noProxy = '<-loopback>;*.corp.contoso.com;*.azure.com;*.microsoft.com'

netsh winhttp set proxy $proxy bypass-list=$noProxy
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('HTTPS_PROXY', $proxy, 'Machine')
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('HTTP_PROXY',  $proxy, 'Machine')
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('NO_PROXY',    $noProxy, 'Machine')

# Git CLI
git config --global http.proxy  $proxy
git config --global https.proxy $proxy

# PowerShell module install via authenticated proxy
$cred = Get-Credential -Message 'Proxy account'
Set-PSResourceRepository -Name PSGallery -Trusted
Install-PSResource -Name <module> `
    -Proxy $proxy -ProxyCredential $cred -Scope AllUsers
```

If the environment is truly air-gapped (no egress at all), bind §0.5 to an
internal-only feed for **every** entry in §0.5.a/b — the procedures in
Phases 1–13 will work unchanged against mirror URLs.

## Phase summaries

Detailed operational PowerShell for each phase is **inlined below**
(Phases 1–13). The Phase summary table here is the navigational index;
Phase 14 (OrgTree Integration) follows after.

| # | Phase | Purpose | Posted §  |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Windows Server 2025 base | Core role install, patch baseline, firewall profile, WinRM lockdown | §4 |
| 2 | IIS install + configuration | URL Rewrite, Application Request Routing, health probes, logging targets | §5 |
| 3 | TLS certificates | ACME or enterprise CA issuance, private key ACL, binding to IIS site | §6 |
| 4 | Gitea install | Service account, D: data volume, `app.ini` canonical values, service registration | §7 |
| 5 | IIS reverse proxy | Rewrite rules for 443→3000, HSTS, request size limits, X-Forwarded-For hand-off | §8 |
| 6 | AD LDAPS authentication | `source-config` in Gitea, LDAPS bind account, search base scoped to OrgPath-governed OUs | §9 |
| 7 | Entra OAuth2/OIDC | App registration, redirect URIs, group claim configuration, auto-provisioning | §10 |
| 8 | Clone UIAO repo | Initial `git clone` from GitHub origin, Gitea mirror configuration, LFS enablement | §11 |
| 9 | Gitea governance hooks | Pre-receive / post-receive hooks for canon validation, signed-commit enforcement, Quarto render triggers | §12 |
| 10 | Intune compliance | Autopilot or manual enrollment, compliance policy scoping, attestation reporting to Graph | §13 |
| 11 | Azure Arc enrollment | Service principal auth, azcmagent connect, policy assignment, Monitor Agent extension | §14 |
| 12 | Backup, replication, DR | `Backup-UIAOGitea` function, Azure Blob upload, active-passive replica, RPO/RTO targets | §15 |
| 13 | Security hardening | CIS baseline, AppLocker, Defender for Servers Plan 2, audit policy, WDAC | §16 |
| **14** | **OrgTree integration** | **OrgPath tagging · AU delegation · dynamic-group Gitea auth · UIAO_163 drift hooks · OrgPath-scoped Intune compliance** | *new — this page* |

## Phase 1 — Windows Server 2025 base hardening

```powershell
# 1.a Patch baseline (Microsoft Update for OS + driver class)
Set-Service -Name wuauserv -StartupType Automatic -Status Running
Install-Module -Name PSWindowsUpdate -Force
Import-Module PSWindowsUpdate
Get-WindowsUpdate -AcceptAll -Install -AutoReboot

# 1.b Firewall — Domain profile only, deny by default
Set-NetFirewallProfile -Profile Domain  -Enabled True -DefaultInboundAction Block -DefaultOutboundAction Allow
Set-NetFirewallProfile -Profile Private,Public -Enabled True -DefaultInboundAction Block

# 1.c Restrict WinRM (Tier-0 administration only)
Set-WSManInstance -ResourceURI 'winrm/config/service' -ValueSet @{ AllowUnencrypted = 'false' }
Set-WSManInstance -ResourceURI 'winrm/config/service/auth' -ValueSet @{ Basic = 'false'; Negotiate = 'true' }
New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'UIAO WinRM 5986 Tier0' -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 5986 `
    -RemoteAddress '10.1.20.0/24' -Action Allow -Profile Domain

# 1.d Disable legacy protocols
Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName SMB1Protocol -NoRestart
Set-SmbServerConfiguration -EncryptData $true -EnableSMB1Protocol $false -Confirm:$false

# 1.e Time sync against the forest PDC
w32tm /config /manualpeerlist:"pdc.corp.contoso.com,0x1" /syncfromflags:manual /update
Restart-Service w32time
```

## Phase 2 — IIS install + configuration

```powershell
# 2.a Roles + features (see §0.4.a). After the call, install the four
#     out-of-band MSIs (URL Rewrite, ARR, HttpPlatformHandler, .NET 8 hosting bundle).
$msi = @(
    @{ Name = 'URL Rewrite 2.1';  Uri = 'https://download.microsoft.com/download/D/8/1/D81E5DD6-1ABB-46B0-9B4B-21894E18B77F/rewrite_amd64_en-US.msi' }
    @{ Name = 'ARR 3.0';          Uri = 'https://download.microsoft.com/download/E/9/8/E9849D6A-020E-47E4-9FD0-A023E99B54EB/requestRouter_amd64.msi' }
    @{ Name = 'HttpPlatformHandler 1.2'; Uri = 'https://download.microsoft.com/download/A/8/0/A80747C3-41BD-45DF-B505-E9710D2744E0/httpPlatformHandler_amd64.msi' }
    @{ Name = '.NET 8 hosting bundle';  Uri = 'https://builds.dotnet.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/Runtime/8.0.6/dotnet-hosting-8.0.6-win.exe' }
)
foreach ($m in $msi) {
    $dst = Join-Path $env:TEMP (Split-Path $m.Uri -Leaf)
    Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $m.Uri -OutFile $dst -UseBasicParsing
    # Verify SHA-256 against canon/release-manifests/platform-server-v1.2.yaml here.
    Start-Process -FilePath $dst -ArgumentList '/quiet','/norestart' -Wait
}
iisreset /restart

# 2.b ARR — enable proxy mode (required for reverse-proxy rewrite rules)
Import-Module IISAdministration
Set-WebConfigurationProperty -PSPath 'MACHINE/WEBROOT/APPHOST' `
    -Filter 'system.webServer/proxy' -Name 'enabled' -Value $true
```

## Phase 3 — TLS certificates

```powershell
# 3.a Request a server certificate from the enterprise CA (template: WebServerV2)
$req = @"
[NewRequest]
Subject = "CN=git.uiao.corp.contoso.com,O=Contoso,L=City,S=ST,C=US"
KeyLength = 4096
KeyAlgorithm = RSA
MachineKeySet = TRUE
Exportable = FALSE
RequestType = PKCS10
KeyUsage = 0xa0
[Extensions]
2.5.29.17 = "{text}"
_continue_ = "dns=git.uiao.corp.contoso.com&"
_continue_ = "dns=git-canon.corp.contoso.com&"
[RequestAttributes]
CertificateTemplate = "WebServerV2"
"@
$req | Out-File "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.inf" -Encoding ascii
certreq -new "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.inf" "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.req"
certreq -submit -config "ca01.corp.contoso.com\Contoso-IssuingCA" `
        "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.req" "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.cer"
certreq -accept "$env:TEMP\uiao-git.cer"

# 3.b ACL the private key to the Gitea service account + IIS app-pool identity
$thumb = (Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My |
    Where-Object Subject -like '*git.uiao*' | Select-Object -First 1).Thumbprint
$keyName = (Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$thumb).PrivateKey.CspKeyContainerInfo.UniqueKeyContainerName
$keyPath = Join-Path "$env:ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\Keys" $keyName
$acl = Get-Acl $keyPath
$acl.AddAccessRule((New-Object Security.AccessControl.FileSystemAccessRule(
    'NT SERVICE\gitea','Read','Allow')))
$acl.AddAccessRule((New-Object Security.AccessControl.FileSystemAccessRule(
    'IIS AppPool\UIAOReverseProxy','Read','Allow')))
Set-Acl -Path $keyPath -AclObject $acl
```

## Phase 4 — Gitea install

```powershell
# 4.a Service account (gMSA preferred for production)
New-ADServiceAccount -Name 'svc-uiao-gitea' `
    -DNSHostName 'uiao-git01.corp.contoso.com' `
    -PrincipalsAllowedToRetrieveManagedPassword 'UIAO-GIT01$' `
    -KerberosEncryptionType AES128,AES256
Install-ADServiceAccount svc-uiao-gitea

# 4.b Layout
$root = 'D:\Gitea'
'data','log','custom','tmp' | ForEach-Object {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Join-Path $root $_) -Force | Out-Null
}
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'D:\GitRepos' -Force | Out-Null  # the actual repo storage
icacls $root /grant 'corp\svc-uiao-gitea$:(OI)(CI)M' /T /C /Q
icacls 'D:\GitRepos' /grant 'corp\svc-uiao-gitea$:(OI)(CI)M' /T /C /Q

# 4.c Download the pinned Gitea binary (verify SHA-256 against the release manifest)
$ver  = '1.21.11'
$uri  = "https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/releases/download/v$ver/gitea-$ver-windows-4.0-amd64.exe"
$exe  = "$root\gitea.exe"
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $uri -OutFile $exe -UseBasicParsing
# Get-FileHash $exe | Select-Object -Expand Hash   # compare to canon/release-manifests

# 4.d Canonical app.ini (only the fields that diverge from defaults — full file
#     lives at src/uiao/canon/platform/gitea/app.ini once committed)
@"
APP_NAME = UIAO Canon
RUN_USER = corp\svc-uiao-gitea$
RUN_MODE = prod
[server]
PROTOCOL          = http
HTTP_ADDR         = 127.0.0.1
HTTP_PORT         = 3000
ROOT_URL          = https://git.uiao.corp.contoso.com/
DISABLE_SSH       = false
SSH_DOMAIN        = git.uiao.corp.contoso.com
SSH_LISTEN_PORT   = 2222
APP_DATA_PATH     = D:\Gitea\data
LFS_START_SERVER  = true
LFS_CONTENT_PATH  = D:\Gitea\data\lfs
[repository]
ROOT              = D:\GitRepos
DEFAULT_BRANCH    = main
[database]
DB_TYPE           = postgres
HOST              = pg01.corp.contoso.com:5432
NAME              = uiao_gitea
USER              = uiao_gitea
PASSWD            = `${SECRET:gitea-db-password}
[security]
INSTALL_LOCK              = true
DISABLE_GIT_HOOKS         = false
IMPORT_LOCAL_PATHS        = false
INTERNAL_TOKEN            = `${SECRET:gitea-internal-token}
[oauth2]
JWT_SECRET                = `${SECRET:gitea-jwt-secret}
[log]
ROOT_PATH = D:\Gitea\log
MODE      = console,file
LEVEL     = Info
[service]
DISABLE_REGISTRATION              = true
REQUIRE_SIGNIN_VIEW                = true
ENABLE_NOTIFY_MAIL                 = false
DEFAULT_KEEP_EMAIL_PRIVATE         = true
"@ | Out-File "$root\custom\conf\app.ini" -Encoding utf8

# 4.e Register as a Windows service under the gMSA
& $exe service install `
    --config "$root\custom\conf\app.ini" `
    --user 'corp\svc-uiao-gitea$' --service-name 'gitea'
Set-Service -Name gitea -StartupType Automatic
Start-Service -Name gitea
```

## Phase 5 — IIS reverse proxy (443 → Gitea :3000)

```powershell
# 5.a Site + binding
Import-Module IISAdministration
$site = 'UIAOReverseProxy'
$pool = 'UIAOReverseProxy'
$thumb = (Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My |
    Where-Object Subject -like '*git.uiao*' | Select-Object -First 1).Thumbprint

New-WebAppPool -Name $pool -Force
Set-ItemProperty "IIS:\AppPools\$pool" -Name managedRuntimeVersion -Value ''
Set-ItemProperty "IIS:\AppPools\$pool" -Name processModel.identityType -Value 'ApplicationPoolIdentity'

New-Website -Name $site -PhysicalPath 'C:\inetpub\wwwroot\uiao-proxy' `
    -ApplicationPool $pool -Port 443 -Ssl -HostHeader 'git.uiao.corp.contoso.com' -Force
New-Item 'C:\inetpub\wwwroot\uiao-proxy' -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null

netsh http add sslcert hostnameport=git.uiao.corp.contoso.com:443 `
    certhash=$thumb appid="{$([guid]::NewGuid())}" certstorename=MY

# 5.b Rewrite rules — forward everything to Gitea on loopback :3000
$rules = @"
<configuration>
  <system.webServer>
    <rewrite>
      <rules>
        <rule name='UIAO-Gitea-Proxy' stopProcessing='true'>
          <match url='(.*)' />
          <action type='Rewrite' url='http://127.0.0.1:3000/{R:1}' />
          <serverVariables>
            <set name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO' value='https' />
            <set name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR'   value='{REMOTE_ADDR}' />
            <set name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST'  value='{HTTP_HOST}' />
          </serverVariables>
        </rule>
      </rules>
      <allowedServerVariables>
        <add name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO' />
        <add name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR' />
        <add name='HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST' />
      </allowedServerVariables>
    </rewrite>
    <httpProtocol>
      <customHeaders>
        <add name='Strict-Transport-Security' value='max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains' />
        <add name='X-Content-Type-Options'    value='nosniff' />
        <add name='X-Frame-Options'           value='DENY' />
      </customHeaders>
    </httpProtocol>
    <security>
      <requestFiltering>
        <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength='524288000' />
      </requestFiltering>
    </security>
  </system.webServer>
</configuration>
"@
$rules | Out-File "C:\inetpub\wwwroot\uiao-proxy\web.config" -Encoding utf8

iisreset /restart
```

## Phase 6 — AD LDAPS authentication (Gitea source-config)

```powershell
# 6.a LDAPS bind account (least-privilege; can read users + group memberships only)
$bindDn = 'CN=svc-uiao-ldap,OU=ServiceAccounts,DC=corp,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$bindPw = Read-Host -AsSecureString -Prompt 'svc-uiao-ldap password'

# 6.b Register the source via the Gitea API (token from Phase 4 admin user)
$token = $env:GITEA_ADMIN_TOKEN
$body  = @{
    type            = 'ldap'
    name            = 'corp-ad-ldaps'
    host            = 'dc01.corp.contoso.com'
    port            = 636
    security_protocol = 'LDAPS'
    skip_verify     = $false
    bind_dn         = $bindDn
    bind_password   = (ConvertFrom-SecureString -SecureString $bindPw -AsPlainText)
    user_search_base = 'OU=Users,OU=ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM,DC=corp,DC=contoso,DC=com'
    filter          = '(&(objectClass=user)(sAMAccountName=%s))'
    admin_filter    = '(memberOf=CN=OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-CanonStewards,OU=Groups,DC=corp,DC=contoso,DC=com)'
    email_attribute = 'mail'
    name_attribute  = 'sAMAccountName'
    surname_attribute = 'sn'
    given_name_attribute = 'givenName'
    is_active       = $true
    is_sync_enabled = $true
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'https://git.uiao.corp.contoso.com/api/v1/admin/sources' `
    -Method Post -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json' `
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "token $token" }
```

## Phase 7 — Entra OAuth2 / OIDC

```powershell
# 7.a Register the app (idempotent guard via displayName lookup)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Application.ReadWrite.All','Directory.ReadWrite.All' -NoWelcome
$app = Get-MgApplication -Filter "displayName eq 'UIAO-Gitea-OIDC'" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (-not $app) {
    $app = New-MgApplication -DisplayName 'UIAO-Gitea-OIDC' `
        -SignInAudience 'AzureADMyOrg' `
        -Web @{ RedirectUris = @('https://git.uiao.corp.contoso.com/user/oauth2/EntraID/callback') } `
        -OptionalClaims @{ IdToken = @(@{ name = 'groups' }) } `
        -GroupMembershipClaims 'SecurityGroup'
    $sp = New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId $app.AppId
}

# 7.b Client secret — store in SecretStore for the Gitea service account
$secret = Add-MgApplicationPassword -ApplicationId $app.Id `
    -PasswordCredential @{ DisplayName = 'Gitea-OIDC'; EndDateTime = (Get-Date).AddYears(1) }
Set-Secret -Name 'gitea-oidc-secret' -SecureStringSecret (ConvertTo-SecureString $secret.SecretText -AsPlainText -Force) `
    -Vault UIAOPlatformVault

# 7.c Register the auth source in Gitea
$oidcBody = @{
    type     = 'oauth2'
    name     = 'EntraID'
    is_active = $true
    oauth2_config = @{
        provider      = 'openidConnect'
        client_id     = $app.AppId
        client_secret = $secret.SecretText
        openid_connect_auto_discovery_url = 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/' +
                                            (Get-MgOrganization).Id + '/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration'
        scopes        = 'openid profile email groups'
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'https://git.uiao.corp.contoso.com/api/v1/admin/sources' `
    -Method Post -Body $oidcBody -ContentType 'application/json' `
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "token $env:GITEA_ADMIN_TOKEN" }
```

## Phase 8 — Clone UIAO repo + configure mirror from GitHub

```powershell
# 8.a Create the org + repo in Gitea, then point it at GitHub upstream as a pull-mirror
$base = 'https://git.uiao.corp.contoso.com/api/v1'
$h    = @{ Authorization = "token $env:GITEA_ADMIN_TOKEN" }

# Org
$null = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$base/orgs" -Method Post -Headers $h -ContentType 'application/json' `
    -Body (@{ username = 'uiao'; visibility = 'private' } | ConvertTo-Json)

# Pull mirror from GitHub canonical origin
$mirror = @{
    clone_addr  = 'https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao.git'
    repo_name   = 'uiao'
    repo_owner  = 'uiao'
    mirror      = $true
    mirror_interval = '15m0s'
    lfs         = $true
    private     = $true
} | ConvertTo-Json
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$base/repos/migrate" -Method Post -Headers $h `
    -ContentType 'application/json' -Body $mirror

# 8.b Optional: enable Git LFS server (already set in app.ini; verify storage path)
Test-Path 'D:\Gitea\data\lfs'
```

## Phase 9 — Gitea governance hooks

The pre-receive / update / post-receive hook bodies are authored in
[`git-server-implementation.qmd` §15 (Phase 12)](./git-server-implementation.qmd)
and apply unchanged to Gitea; the only path difference is the hook destination —
in Gitea, server-side hooks live in
`D:\GitRepos\uiao\uiao.git\hooks\` (or are managed via the Gitea web UI under
*Settings → Webhooks → Git Hooks*). Mandatory rules:

| Hook | Rule | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `pre-receive` | Reject pushes to `main` unless author ∈ `OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-CanonStewards` | `git-server-implementation.qmd` §15 Rule 1 |
| `pre-receive` | Reject any commit containing `FOUO` / `For Official Use Only` markings | §15 Rule 2 |
| `pre-receive` | Validate canon-file YAML frontmatter (document_id, version, status, classification, owner, boundary) | §15 Rule 3 |
| `pre-receive` | Reject canon files not matching `^UIAO_\d{3}_[\w]+_v\d+\.\d+\.md$` | §15 Rule 4 |
| `pre-receive` | Reject unsigned `.ps1` files under `scripts/` (Authenticode + WDAC requirement) | new, Phase 0.6 |
| `update`      | Enforce branch-naming allowlist: `main, develop, feature/*, bugfix/*, hotfix/*, canon/*, release/*` | §15 |
| `post-receive` | Log push + emit `repository_dispatch` to GitHub Actions for canonical CI mirroring | §15 |

Sign every hook with the Phase 0.6 code-signing certificate before deploying.

## Phase 10 — Intune compliance

```powershell
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'DeviceManagementConfiguration.ReadWrite.All','Group.ReadWrite.All' -NoWelcome

# 10.a Compliance policy — Tier-0 baseline (Defender for Servers Plan 2 active, etc.)
$policy = @{
    '@odata.type'                       = '#microsoft.graph.windows10CompliancePolicy'
    displayName                         = 'UIAO-GIT-ServerCompliance-v2'
    description                         = 'Tier-0 compliance for the UIAO platform server (OrgPath-scoped)'
    osMinimumVersion                    = '10.0.26100'
    secureBootEnabled                   = $true
    bitLockerEnabled                    = $true
    codeIntegrityEnabled                = $true
    storageRequireEncryption            = $true
    activeFirewallRequired              = $true
    antivirusRequired                   = $true
    rtpEnabled                          = $true
    tpmRequired                         = $true
    defenderEnabled                     = $true
    deviceCompliancePolicyScript        = $null
}
$created = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/deviceCompliancePolicies' `
    -Body ($policy | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)

# 10.b OrgPath-scoped assignment — see Phase 14.5 for the dynamic group rule
$assign = @{
    assignments = @(@{
        target = @{
            '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.groupAssignmentTarget'
            groupId       = (Get-MgGroup -Filter "displayName eq 'OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Devices'").Id
        }
    })
}
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/deviceManagement/deviceCompliancePolicies/$($created.id)/assign" `
    -Body ($assign | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)
```

## Phase 11 — Azure Arc enrollment

```powershell
# 11.a Service-principal sign-in (no interactive creds on a Tier-0 server)
$tenant   = (Get-Secret -Name 'arc-tenant-id' -AsPlainText)
$appId    = (Get-Secret -Name 'arc-onboarding-spn-appid' -AsPlainText)
$appSecret= (Get-Secret -Name 'arc-onboarding-spn-secret' -AsPlainText)
$subId    = (Get-Secret -Name 'arc-subscription-id' -AsPlainText)
$rg       = 'rg-uiao-governance'
$loc      = 'eastus'

# 11.b Install Arc agent + connect
$msi = "$env:TEMP\AzureConnectedMachineAgent.msi"
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri 'https://aka.ms/AzureConnectedMachineAgent' -OutFile $msi -UseBasicParsing
Start-Process msiexec -ArgumentList "/i $msi /qn /norestart" -Wait

& "$env:ProgramFiles\AzureConnectedMachineAgent\azcmagent.exe" connect `
    --service-principal-id     $appId `
    --service-principal-secret $appSecret `
    --tenant-id  $tenant `
    --subscription-id $subId `
    --resource-group  $rg `
    --location $loc `
    --resource-name 'UIAO-GIT01' `
    --tags 'OrgPath=ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM' 'DeviceRole=GitServer' 'DeviceTier=Tier0'

# 11.c Azure Monitor Agent (for SIEM forwarding in Phase 13)
$ama = @{
    name      = 'AzureMonitorWindowsAgent'
    publisher = 'Microsoft.Azure.Monitor'
    type      = 'AzureMonitorWindowsAgent'
    location  = $loc
}
New-AzConnectedMachineExtension @ama `
    -ResourceGroupName $rg `
    -MachineName 'UIAO-GIT01'
```

## Phase 12 — Backup, replication, DR

```powershell
function Backup-UIAOGitea {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [string]$GiteaRoot   = 'D:\Gitea',
        [string]$RepoRoot    = 'D:\GitRepos',
        [string]$StagingDir  = 'E:\Backups\Staging',
        [string]$StorageAcct = 'stuiaogovernance',
        [string]$Container   = 'gitea-backups',
        [int]   $RetainDays  = 30
    )

    $ts  = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmm'
    $stg = Join-Path $StagingDir "uiao-gitea-$ts"
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $stg -Force | Out-Null

    # 12.a Quiesce Gitea, snapshot DB + repo storage, restart
    Stop-Service gitea
    & "$GiteaRoot\gitea.exe" dump --config "$GiteaRoot\custom\conf\app.ini" `
        --file (Join-Path $stg 'gitea-dump.zip')
    Compress-Archive -Path "$RepoRoot\*" -DestinationPath (Join-Path $stg 'repos.zip') -CompressionLevel Optimal
    Start-Service gitea

    # 12.b Upload (managed identity preferred; SAS shown for clarity)
    Connect-AzAccount -Identity | Out-Null
    $ctx = (Get-AzStorageAccount -ResourceGroupName 'rg-uiao-governance' -Name $StorageAcct).Context
    Get-ChildItem $stg | ForEach-Object {
        Set-AzStorageBlobContent -File $_.FullName -Container $Container `
            -Blob "$ts/$($_.Name)" -Context $ctx -Force
    }

    # 12.c Retention — delete blobs older than $RetainDays
    Get-AzStorageBlob -Container $Container -Context $ctx |
        Where-Object { $_.LastModified -lt (Get-Date).AddDays(-$RetainDays) } |
        Remove-AzStorageBlob -Force

    Remove-Item $stg -Recurse -Force
}

# Schedule nightly at 02:00
$action  = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute 'pwsh.exe' `
    -Argument '-NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy AllSigned -Command "Import-Module UIAOPlatform; Backup-UIAOGitea"'
$trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -Daily -At 02:00
Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName 'UIAO-GiteaBackup' -Action $action -Trigger $trigger `
    -User 'NT SERVICE\gitea' -RunLevel Highest -Force
```

RPO target: 24 h (nightly snapshot). RTO target: 4 h (cold restore from
latest backup blob into the passive replica + DNS cutover).

## Phase 13 — Security hardening

```powershell
# 13.a CIS Level 2 baseline via the Microsoft Security Compliance Toolkit GPO
#     (download separately; apply with LGPO.exe from the toolkit)
& "$env:ProgramFiles\LGPO\LGPO.exe" /g "$env:TEMP\CIS-WS2025-L2-GPO"

# 13.b AppLocker — allow signed UIAO scripts; default-deny everything else
$xml = @'
<AppLockerPolicy Version="1">
  <RuleCollection Type="Script" EnforcementMode="Enabled">
    <FilePublisherRule Id="uiao-signed-scripts" Name="UIAO signed scripts" Action="Allow" UserOrGroupSid="S-1-1-0">
      <Conditions>
        <FilePublisherCondition PublisherName="CN=UIAO Canon Signing" ProductName="*" BinaryName="*">
          <BinaryVersionRange LowSection="*" HighSection="*" />
        </FilePublisherCondition>
      </Conditions>
    </FilePublisherRule>
  </RuleCollection>
</AppLockerPolicy>
'@
$xml | Out-File "$env:TEMP\uiao-applocker.xml" -Encoding utf8
Set-AppLockerPolicy -XmlPolicy "$env:TEMP\uiao-applocker.xml" -Merge
Start-Service -Name AppIDSvc -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Set-Service -Name AppIDSvc -StartupType Automatic

# 13.c WDAC — enforce code integrity for the signed-script policy
$policyPath = "$env:TEMP\UIAO-WDAC.xml"
New-CIPolicy -FilePath $policyPath -Level Publisher `
    -ScanPath 'D:\GitRepos\uiao\scripts' -UserPEs -NoShadowCopy
Add-SignerRule -FilePath $policyPath -CertificatePath 'C:\Certs\UIAOCanonSigning.cer' -User -Update
Set-RuleOption -FilePath $policyPath -Option 0   # Enabled:UMCI
Set-RuleOption -FilePath $policyPath -Option 3 -Delete  # remove Audit Mode
ConvertFrom-CIPolicy -XmlFilePath $policyPath -BinaryFilePath "$env:TEMP\UIAO-WDAC.cip"
Copy-Item "$env:TEMP\UIAO-WDAC.cip" 'C:\Windows\System32\CodeIntegrity\SiPolicy.p7b' -Force
Restart-Computer -Force

# 13.d Defender for Servers Plan 2 + SIEM forwarding via Arc + Azure Monitor
#     (Plan 2 is enabled at the subscription level; agent is from Phase 11)
Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $false `
    -CloudBlockLevel 'High' -MAPSReporting 'Advanced' -SubmitSamplesConsent 'SendSafeSamples'

# 13.e Audit policy
auditpol /set /category:'Logon/Logoff'         /success:enable /failure:enable
auditpol /set /category:'Object Access'        /success:enable /failure:enable
auditpol /set /category:'Privilege Use'        /success:enable /failure:enable
auditpol /set /category:'System'               /success:enable /failure:enable
auditpol /set /category:'Account Management'   /success:enable /failure:enable
auditpol /set /category:'Policy Change'        /success:enable /failure:enable
```

## Phase 14 — OrgTree Integration

Phase 14 registers the platform server inside the Modernization canon. Five
sub-phases, each citing a MOD_* appendix rather than duplicating it.

### 14.1 OrgPath tagging

Every governed object — users, groups, devices, servers — carries an
OrgPath in `extensionAttribute1`. The platform server sits in the IT
Infrastructure Platform branch.

```
OrgPath:  ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM
Codebook: UIAO_151 (OrgPath Codebook) — validation regex ^ORG(-[A-Z]{2,6}){0,4}$
```

Set the OrgPath on the Entra device object and mirror it to Azure tags on
the Arc-projected resource:

```powershell
# Entra device object — set extensionAttribute1
Update-MgDevice -DeviceId $serverDeviceId `
  -ExtensionAttributes @{ extensionAttribute1 = "ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM" }

# Azure Arc resource — mirror to tags (extends Posted §14.6)
az connectedmachine update `
  --name "UIAO-GIT01" `
  --resource-group "rg-uiao-governance" `
  --tags OrgPath=ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM `
         OrgPathBranch=ORG-IT-INF `
         OrgPathDivision=ORG-IT `
         DeviceRole=GitServer `
         DeviceTier=Tier0
```

The codebook entry `ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM` must exist in UIAO_151 before this
runs; missing entries are rejected by UIAO_158 (OrgPath JSON Schema) and flagged
as **Format Drift** by UIAO_163.

**Canonical references:** UIAO_151 (codebook), UIAO_153 (attribute mapping),
UIAO_158 (JSON schema).

### 14.2 Administrative Unit for server-admin delegation

Server administration is delegated via an AU scoped to the Platform team's
OrgPath. The AU is a Tier-3 Department-level unit per UIAO_154's three-tier
delegation model.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| AU name | `AU-ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM` |
| Tier | 3 (Department) |
| Membership rule | `(user.extensionAttribute1 -startsWith "ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM")` |
| Restricted | Yes |
| Scoped roles | Authentication Administrator · User Administrator · Helpdesk Administrator |

Create and populate via MS Graph:

```powershell
# Create the AU (idempotent — pattern per UIAO_154)
$au = New-MgDirectoryAdministrativeUnit -BodyParameter @{
  displayName          = "AU-ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM"
  description          = "Platform infrastructure team — scoped delegation"
  visibility           = "HiddenMembership"
  isMemberManagementRestricted = $true
}

# Assign scoped role (pattern from UIAO_154 Role Assignment Matrix)
New-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleAssignment -BodyParameter @{
  roleDefinitionId = "<Authentication Administrator roleTemplateId>"
  principalId      = "<OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Admins group objectId>"
  directoryScopeId = "/administrativeUnits/$($au.Id)"
}
```

Global roles (Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator) are
explicitly **not** granted here; platform administrators have no tenant-wide
scope.

**Canonical references:** UIAO_154 (Delegation Matrix — §Tier 3 Department AUs),
UIAO_167 (SLA Escalation Playbooks — for permission-review cadence).

### 14.3 Gitea authorization via dynamic groups

Gitea's team model maps to OrgTree dynamic groups rather than hand-maintained
membership. Three groups govern the UIAO canon repo:

| Gitea role | Dynamic group | Membership rule (UIAO_152 §Pattern 1) |
|---|---|---|
| Owner | `OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-CanonStewards` | `(user.extensionAttribute1 -eq "ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM-STW")` |
| Write | `OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Contributors` | `(user.extensionAttribute1 -startsWith "ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM")` |
| Read | `OrgTree-IT-INF-Users` | `(user.extensionAttribute1 -startsWith "ORG-IT-INF")` |

Because the Entra OIDC provider (Posted §10) emits group claims, Gitea sees
dynamic-group membership as native team membership — no manual Gitea user
admin is required. A user's move in the HR feed propagates through the
dynamic-group rules and arrives as a Gitea permission change within the
Entra group-recalculation window (typically < 15 minutes).

Group names must conform to `OrgTree-[SCOPE]-[PURPOSE]` per UIAO_152 §Naming
Convention. Non-conforming groups are Phantom Drift.

**Canonical references:** UIAO_152 (Dynamic Group Library — §Naming, §Pattern 1
Branch Query), UIAO_155 (Governance Workflow Catalog — joiner/mover/leaver
recalculation).

### 14.4 UIAO_163 drift detection hook

The platform server emits its canonical state to UIAO_163 on a schedule. Five
drift signals are reported; any non-zero count opens an SLA ticket per UIAO_167.

| Signal | Detection | Drift category |
|---|---|---|
| OrgPath mismatch (Entra vs. Azure tag) | Compare `extensionAttribute1` to Arc `OrgPath` tag | Value Drift |
| OrgPath not in codebook | Regex + UIAO_151 lookup | Format Drift / Orphan |
| AU membership lost | `Get-MgDirectoryAdministrativeUnitMember` returns empty | Hierarchy Drift |
| Dynamic group missing from Gitea team | Graph group claim vs. Gitea team list | Phantom Drift |
| Arc agent heartbeat stale > 24h | `azcmagent show` lastHeartbeat | Operational (non-drift alert) |

Reporter script runs as a scheduled task under the service principal used
for Arc (no interactive credentials on the box):

```powershell
# Phase 14.4 — UIAO_163 drift reporter (skeleton)
$state = [ordered]@{
  deviceId      = $env:ComputerName
  orgPath       = (Get-ArcTag -Key OrgPath)
  expectedPath  = (Get-EntraDeviceAttr -DeviceId $deviceId -Name extensionAttribute1)
  auId          = (Get-MgDirectoryAdministrativeUnit -Filter "displayName eq 'AU-ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM'").Id
  gitEAuthTeams = (Invoke-GiteaApi -Endpoint "/orgs/uiao/teams" | Select-Object -ExpandProperty name)
  timestamp     = (Get-Date -Format o)
}
Submit-DriftReport -Engine UIAO_163 -Payload $state
```

Full reporter and schema are canonical in UIAO_163; the Posted §17 Validation
and Smoke Testing section becomes a consumer of this payload.

**Canonical references:** UIAO_163 (Drift Detection Engine Specification — §Five
Drift Categories), UIAO_167 (SLA Escalation Playbooks), UIAO_174 (Telemetry Model).

### 14.5 OrgPath-scoped Intune compliance targeting

Phase 10 in the Posted guide applies a flat compliance policy. Phase 14.5
scopes that policy to devices whose `OrgPath` device-attribute starts with
the Platform branch.

```
Device dynamic group:  OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Devices
Membership rule:       (device.extensionAttribute1 -startsWith "ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM")
Intune policy:         "UIAO-GIT-ServerCompliance-v2"
Assignment scope:      OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Devices (include)
Exclusion:             none
```

Benefits: policy edits for the Platform branch don't touch other IT
subgroups; joiner-replacement hardware inherits compliance targeting the
moment its OrgPath is set; the drift engine flags any platform device
whose OrgPath does not resolve to this group.

**Canonical references:** UIAO_152 (dynamic group patterns for device objects),
UIAO_154 (scoping), UIAO_171 (Multi-Cloud Boundary Model — confirms Intune falls
inside GCC-Moderate).

## Validation

Phase 14 passes when:

- [ ] `ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM` is present in UIAO_151 codebook YAML.
- [ ] The Entra device object for `UIAO-GIT01` has `extensionAttribute1 =
      ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM`.
- [ ] Azure Arc tags on `UIAO-GIT01` include `OrgPath=ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM`.
- [ ] `AU-ORG-IT-INF-PLATFORM` exists, is restricted, and contains the
      Platform Stewards and Contributors groups as members.
- [ ] The three Gitea teams map 1:1 to the dynamic groups in §14.3.
- [ ] A manual `Submit-DriftReport` returns zero drift findings.
- [ ] Intune compliance policy `UIAO-GIT-ServerCompliance-v2` is assigned
      only to `OrgTree-IT-INF-PLATFORM-Devices`.

## Canonical references

Every appendix is listed in [the OrgTree canon page](../../modernization/orgtree.html).
Source markdown lives at `src/uiao/modernization/orgtree/` in the monorepo.

| Appendix | Title | In-site anchor | Used by phase |
|---|---|---|---|
| UIAO_151 | OrgPath Codebook | [codebook](../../modernization/codebook.html) | 14.1 |
| UIAO_152 | Dynamic Group Library | [dynamic-groups](../../modernization/dynamic-groups.html) | 14.3, 14.5 |
| UIAO_153 | Attribute Mapping Table | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.1 |
| UIAO_154 | Delegation Matrix (AUs + Roles) | [delegation](../../modernization/delegation.html) | 14.2, 14.5 |
| UIAO_155 | Governance Workflow Catalog | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.3 |
| UIAO_156 | Migration Runbook (OU → Entra) | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | context |
| UIAO_158 | OrgPath JSON Schema | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.1 |
| UIAO_163 | Drift Detection Engine | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.4 |
| UIAO_167 | SLA Escalation Playbooks | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.2, 14.4 |
| UIAO_171 | Multi-Cloud Boundary Model | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.5 |
| UIAO_174 | Governance Telemetry Model | [orgtree](../../modernization/orgtree.html) | 14.4 |

## Supersession and provenance

This page supersedes the OrgTree-related fragments of the Posted v1.0 guide
(specifically the 5-line §14.6 "Apply Azure Tags for OrgPath Alignment").
Phases 1–13 continue to reference the Posted runbook as the operational
source; Phase 14 is authored here and inherits from the MOD canon.

- **Posted source:** `UIAO_SBG_001 v1.0` — `UIAO Platform Server Build
  Guide — Windows Server 2025 with Gitea and IIS.docx` (1,864 lines).
  **Currently absent** from `inbox/Posted/`; Phase 1–13 inlined above is the
  interim operational authority.
- **Canon anchors:** `src/uiao/canon/document-registry.yaml`
  entries UIAO_151, UIAO_152, UIAO_153, UIAO_154, UIAO_155, UIAO_156,
  UIAO_158, UIAO_163, UIAO_167, UIAO_171, UIAO_174 (renamed from
  UIAO_151–UIAO_174 per ADR-060).
- **ADR dependency:** ADR-041 (UIAO Git Infrastructure — Self-Hosted Git on
  Windows Server 2025 + IIS). ADR-041 is currently `status: draft` in
  `src/uiao/canon/adr/`; the Gitea-behind-IIS decision (Option B) is recorded
  there. Earlier revisions of this page mis-cited the decision as ADR-001;
  ADR-001 is `HAADJ Deprecated — Entra ID Join as Sole Device Join Target`
  and is unrelated to Git infrastructure.
- **Boundary:** GCC-Moderate for M365 SaaS interactions; IaaS (Arc, Azure
  tags, Log Analytics) per ADR-041's commercial-FedRAMP exception.

## Appendix A — Option A vs Option B (architectural reconciliation)

ADR-041 evaluates two architectures for self-hosting the UIAO Git
infrastructure on Windows Server 2025 + IIS. Both are documented in this
repo; only one is canon.

| Aspect | Option A — IIS + `git-http-backend` (CGI) | **Option B — Gitea behind IIS reverse proxy (CANON)** |
|---|---|---|
| Git transport | `git-http-backend.exe` invoked via IIS CGI | Gitea native HTTP/2 on `127.0.0.1:3000` |
| Auth | IIS Windows Authentication (Kerberos + NTLM) | Gitea AD LDAPS + Entra OIDC, with IIS terminating TLS |
| Web UI | None — Git CLI only | Gitea web UI, REST API, issues, PRs, OAuth apps |
| Hooks | Server-side `pre-receive` / `update` / `post-receive` in the bare repo | Same plus Gitea-managed hook chain + webhook fan-out |
| OrgTree integration | Manual — AD group → IIS role mapping | Native — Entra group claims map to Gitea teams via Phase 7 / 14.3 |
| LFS | Manual config; requires extra IIS handler | Built-in (`LFS_START_SERVER = true` in app.ini, Phase 4.d) |
| Drift / SLA hooks | Custom PowerShell | Native Gitea webhooks → UIAO_163 emitter (Phase 14.4) |
| Reference doc | [`git-server-implementation.qmd`](./git-server-implementation.qmd) | this page |

**Decision recorded in ADR-041:** Option B is canon. Option A is retained
in [`git-server-implementation.qmd`](./git-server-implementation.qmd) as a
lab-mode shortcut and as the operational source of the hook bodies
referenced from Phase 9 above. Do not deploy Option A to production without
a new ADR superseding ADR-041.

When ADR-041 flips from `status: draft` to `ACCEPTED`, this appendix becomes
the navigation surface from which operators are pointed to the canonical
Option B procedure; the Option A guide will carry a permanent
"superseded for production use" banner.
